api/spec/packages/aip.purpose: Root package for the v3 AIP TypeSpec spec: compiles domain operation interfaces into api/v3/openapi.yaml via two root namespace files (openmeter.tsp, konnect.tsp), runs post-processing (flatten-allof.mjs), and enforces naming/structural constraints via a custom linter (lib/). All route binding, tag metadata, and security schemes live here; domain definitions do not.

patterns.name: Two-step compile pipeline
patterns.description: tsp compile produces OpenAPI YAML, then scripts/flatten-allof.mjs normalises allOf schemas. Both steps run via `pnpm compile` (package.json). Never hand-edit the output.
patterns.example: pnpm compile in api/spec/packages/aip runs both steps sequentially

patterns.name: Route and tag binding at root only
patterns.description: @route and @tag are declared exclusively in src/openmeter.tsp and src/konnect.tsp, never inside domain sub-folder operation files.

patterns.name: Security via @useRef to external YAML
patterns.description: Security schemes are TypeSpec model stubs with @useRef pointing to external YAML fragments, not inline credential definitions.

patterns.name: Domain ops imported via index.tsp barrels
patterns.description: Each domain sub-folder exposes an index.tsp; root files import only the barrel, not individual operation files.

patterns.name: Custom linter via createRule factory
patterns.description: All lint rules in lib/rules/ use the createRule factory and are registered in lib/index.js $linter. Error severity blocks gen-api; warning does not.
patterns.example: lib/rules/casing.js exports createRule({ name: 'casing', ... }); registered in lib/index.js

patterns.name: omit-unreachable-types: true in tspconfig
patterns.description: tspconfig.yaml sets omit-unreachable-types: true so only reachable schemas appear in the emitted OpenAPI.

patterns.name: PascalCase acronym exceptions in utils.js
patterns.description: Accepted uppercase acronyms (OAuth2, URL, API, UI, ID) live exclusively in lib/rules/utils.js pascalCaseExceptions; never add them inline in rule files.

key_file_guides.role: Compilation entry point; imports openmeter.tsp and konnect.tsp
key_file_guides.watch_for: Missing import here silently drops a namespace from output

key_file_guides.role: Binds all domain interfaces to routes and tags for the OpenMeter variant
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding @tagMetadata for a new domain import is mandatory or the tag description is absent from generated OpenAPI

key_file_guides.role: Konnect variant root; overrides security and some sub-routes
key_file_guides.watch_for: Inline security scheme bodies here duplicate config; use @useRef instead

key_file_guides.role: Post-processes emitted YAML to move sibling properties into allOf branches
key_file_guides.watch_for: Removing x-flatten-allOf marker check causes infinite re-run loops; changing YAML_OPTIONS.lineWidth from 0 creates spurious diffs

key_file_guides.role: Registers all linter rules via defineLinter/$linter export
key_file_guides.watch_for: A new rule file has zero effect until its export is added to the rules array here

key_file_guides.role: Centralised casing helpers and acronym exception list
key_file_guides.watch_for: Duplicating regex or exceptions in individual rule files causes divergence

key_file_guides.role: Compiler config: emitter output dir, omit-unreachable-types, linter extends
key_file_guides.watch_for: omit-unreachable-types must stay true; linter extends must reference @openmeter/api-spec-aip/all

anti_patterns: Declaring @route or @tag inside a domain sub-folder's operations.tsp — routing is bound only in openmeter.tsp and konnect.tsp
Hand-editing api/v3/openapi.yaml — always regenerate via `make gen-api`
Defining a lint rule without registering it in lib/index.js — the rule has no effect
Adding new accepted PascalCase acronyms anywhere other than pascalCaseExceptions in lib/rules/utils.js
Adding a domain import in openmeter.tsp without a matching @tagMetadata declaration

decisions.decision: Two root namespace files (openmeter.tsp, konnect.tsp) instead of one parameterised file
decisions.rationale: The two variants differ in security schemes and some sub-route overrides; keeping them as separate files avoids conditional logic in TypeSpec which has no preprocessor

decisions.decision: Post-processing flatten-allof.mjs runs after tsp compile
decisions.rationale: TypeSpec emitter produces allOf schemas with sibling properties that confuse SDK generators; normalisation must happen outside the compiler to avoid patching the upstream emitter

decisions.decision: Custom linter with error vs warning severity split
decisions.rationale: Only friendlyName (SDK-visible names) and structurally breaking rules use error severity to block gen-api; stylistic rules use warning to surface guidance without blocking developer flow

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new domain to the v3 spec

api/spec/packages/legacy.purpose: TypeSpec source for the v1/v2 OpenMeter REST API and OpenMeter Cloud API; compiles to api/openapi.yaml and api/openapi.cloud.yaml plus the Python SDK. src/ is the composition root with shared primitives; cloud/ is the cloud-variant entry point. New v1 domain features go here; v3 changes go to api/spec/packages/aip instead.

patterns.name: main.tsp as sole compilation entry point
patterns.description: All sub-domain .tsp files must be imported into src/cloud/main.tsp (or src/main.tsp for client). Unlisted files are silently excluded from the compiled spec.

patterns.name: @friendlyName on every model, enum, union, interface
patterns.description: Controls the OpenAPI schema name; required on all types to produce deterministic SDK class names. linter enforces this at error severity.

patterns.name: @visibility lifecycle decorators on model fields
patterns.description: Use Lifecycle.Read, Lifecycle.Create, Lifecycle.Update to control which fields appear in which request/response shapes from a single model definition.

patterns.name: REST resource templates for CRUD request bodies
patterns.description: Use TypeSpec.Rest.Resource.ResourceCreateModel<T>, ResourceReplaceModel<T>, ResourceCreateOrUpdateModel<T> to derive request bodies; avoids ad-hoc types and ensures visibility filtering.

patterns.name: Shared primitives in types.tsp, never re-declared in sub-domains
patterns.description: ULID, DateTime, Key, Resource, ResourceTimestamps, CadencedResource and pagination models live in src/types.tsp under the OpenMeter namespace; importing them avoids duplicate schema definitions.
patterns.example: // In a sub-domain file: use OpenMeter.ULID, not a local re-declaration

patterns.name: Filter models with @extension("x-omitempty", true) on all operator fields
patterns.description: Filter types in filter.tsp mark every operator property with @extension("x-omitempty", true) so absent filters are excluded from serialised queries.

patterns.name: @operationId on every interface operation
patterns.description: Explicit camelCase verb+noun operationId prevents non-deterministic SDK function names.

key_file_guides.role: Cloud-variant compilation entry point; imports all sub-domain files
key_file_guides.watch_for: New sub-domain file not imported here is silently excluded from api/openapi.cloud.yaml

key_file_guides.role: Client-emit entry point for Python SDK generation
key_file_guides.watch_for: Separate from cloud/main.tsp; both must be kept in sync for new domains

key_file_guides.role: Shared primitive types: ULID, DateTime, Key, Resource, pagination models
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never re-declare these in sub-domain files; import from this file instead

key_file_guides.role: OpenMeterError and error union types; @error decorator required
key_file_guides.watch_for: Extending Error without @error breaks OpenAPI error schema generation

key_file_guides.role: Generic filter operator models; all fields must have @extension("x-omitempty", true)
key_file_guides.watch_for: Missing x-omitempty causes empty filter fields to be serialised as null in query strings

key_file_guides.role: Registers all v1 linter rules via defineLinter
key_file_guides.watch_for: New rule file has zero effect without registration here

key_file_guides.role: Two configs: OpenAPI emit and Python SDK emit respectively
key_file_guides.watch_for: Both extend @openmeter/api-spec-legacy/all linter; changing emitter-output-dir breaks `make gen-api` path expectations

anti_patterns: Defining new domain models directly in root-level .tsp files instead of a sub-folder with its own main.tsp
Re-declaring primitive types (ULID, DateTime, Key, Resource) in sub-domain files — they already exist in types.tsp
Using `extends Error` without the @error decorator — breaks OpenAPI error schema generation
Omitting @operationId on interface operations — causes non-deterministic generated SDK function names
Adding new sub-domain files without registering them in cloud/main.tsp and src/main.tsp imports

decisions.decision: Two separate tspconfig files (tspconfig.yaml for OpenAPI, tspconfig.client.yaml for Python SDK)
decisions.rationale: OpenAPI and Python SDK emitters need different output directories and emitter options; a single config cannot parameterise both without overriding emitter-output-dir per run

decisions.decision: Shared primitive types centralised in types.tsp with Resource base models
decisions.rationale: Keeps schema naming deterministic across all sub-domains and prevents duplicate $ref targets in generated OpenAPI that confuse SDK generators

decisions.decision: friendlyNameRule is error-severity (blocks gen-api); all other rules are warnings
decisions.rationale: @friendlyName controls SDK class names; a missing or wrong name produces irreversible public API breakage, justifying a hard block on compilation

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new v1 resource (e.g. Widget) with CRUD operations
